verdicts.issue: minor: missing explicit year field; only date = {2018-10-02} present. Quarto/citeproc usually handles this, but explicit year = {2018} is the convention used elsewhere in this bib.

year mismatch with key: key year is 2015 (the ICLR conference year) but year field = {2014} (the arXiv preprint year). The intent of having two keys (kingma2014adam vs kingma2015adam) is preprint vs ICLR; the body of kingma2015adam should report year = {2015}. Currently both entries have year = {2014}, defeating the purpose of the duplicate key. Also booktitle = {International Conference on Learning Representations} should be expanded with (ICLR) per venue-expansion rule.

suggested_fix.note: Fix in-place: set year = {2015}; expand booktitle to {International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR)}; remove the journal = {ICLR} field (it's a conference, the booktitle is correct). No .qmd propagation.

verdicts.issue: minor: missing explicit year field; only date = {2021-09-17} present.
minor: key uses CamelCase (Kocher2011Intro); inconsistent with the rest of the bib's lowercase convention. Not a functional bug — citations resolve — but a normalization candidate.

minor: key uses CamelCase capital K; key year 2018 vs paper IEEE S&P year 2019 (2019 IEEE Symposium on Security and Privacy). 1-year diff is silent per spec, but the entry's year = {2019} is the official conference year — the key prefix 2018 likely refers to the original disclosure (Jan 2018). Fine as-is.

year field = {2008} but the paper appears in the 2009 issue of Data Mining and Knowledge Discovery (Vol 18 No 1, 2009; DOI 10.1007/s10618-008-0114-1). The key year 2009 is correct; the body year should be 2009. Currently key year 2009 != year field 2008.

suggested_fix.note: Fix in-place: set year = {2009}; remove month = jul. Per Springer the issue is Volume 18 (2009). No .qmd propagation.

verdicts.issue: minor: missing explicit year field; only date = {2016-10-08}. Author surname diacritics (Kone\v{c}n\'{y}) fold to 'konecny' which matches the key prefix.

wrong arXiv ID and wrong author list. Title 'Machine Learning Fleet Efficiency: ...' is real but the actual arXiv ID is 2502.06982 (Feb 2025), not 2410.02271 (which doesn't resolve to this title). The actual paper's first author is Wongpanich, A.; the bib entry lists 'Kumar, S. D.' as first author. Year is 2025 (Feb), not 2024. Multiple silent corruptions.

suggested_fix.note: Fix: replace arXiv ID with 2502.06982; update author list to include Wongpanich, Arissa and Oguntebi, Tayo and Paredes, Jose Baiocchi and Wang, Yu Emma and Phothilimthana, Phitchaya Mangpo and Mitra, Ritwika and Zhou, Zongwei and Kumar, Naveen and Reddi, Vijay Janapa; set year = {2025}. Then rename key to wongpanich2025fleet (or kumar2025fleet if Kumar is preferred as conceptual lead) and propagate the citation to all .qmd files referencing [@kumar2024machine].

verdicts.issue: minor: missing explicit year field; only date = {2016-07-08}. Duplicate with kurakin2017adversarial (same paper, different keys for arXiv vs ICLR Workshop).
key year 2017 (ICLR Workshop year) vs body year = {2016} (arXiv year). 1-year diff acceptable per spec; together with kurakin2016adversarial they support both citation styles in prose.
minor: missing explicit year field; only date = {2023-06-26}.
missing pages; ICML 2024 PMLR proceedings has page numbers — should be added if available.
minor: missing explicit year field; only date = {2018-01-19}.

key surname mismatch: prefix 'lai' but author = {Warden, Pete and Situnayake, Daniel}. The book is the Warden & Situnayake O'Reilly TinyML book (2019/2020). 'lai' is not a corporate identifier — looks like a copy-paste leftover from lai2018cmsis or similar. Citations resolve but render misleadingly as Lai when the actual authors are Warden and Situnayake.

suggested_fix.note: Rename @book{lai2020tinyml, ...} to @book{warden2020tinyml, ...} and rewrite all [@lai2020tinyml] / @lai2020tinyml citations in book/quarto/contents/**/*.qmd. Add isbn = {978-1492052043} and consider year = {2019} (O'Reilly print release) vs 2020 (broader availability).

verdicts.issue: minor: missing explicit year field; only the journal Vol/Issue (Science Vol 382 Issue 6677) and DOI imply year 2023.
minor: missing explicit year field; the Advanced Science Vol 8 No 12 = 2021 (DOI confirms).
minor: missing explicit year field; only Electronics Vol 11 No 18 (2022) and DOI imply year.
missing pages — ICLR papers don't have traditional page numbers (OpenReview format), so OK. Booktitle should be expanded to include (ICLR) per venue-expansion rule.
booktitle = {Proceedings of the 40th International Conference on Machine Learning} — should be expanded with (ICML) per venue-expansion rule.
journal/booktitle should expand 'Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems' to include (NeurIPS) per venue-expansion rule. Pages 9459-9474 present, OK.
duplicate of li2014scaling — same paper (Mu Li et al., OSDI 2014). Both intentionally exist for different cite-key conventions in prose; consider consolidating.
duplicate of li2014parameter — same paper (Mu Li et al., OSDI 2014). Consider consolidating into one key.
minor: missing explicit year field; only date = {2017-11-12}. Booktitle SC17 (no page numbers natural).

duplicate of li2020federated_vol2 (same DOI 10.1109/msp.2020.2975749). Also: key uses CamelCase capital L (inconsistent with bib convention). Consider consolidating both into one canonical lowercase key.

key contains underscore '_vol2' suffix and is a duplicate of Li2020Federated. The '_vol2' suffix suggests this was created during a vol1/vol2 merge to disambiguate, but the entry is identical. Also missing explicit year field (only date = {2020-05}).

suggested_fix.note: Delete this duplicate entry; rewrite all [@li2020federated_vol2] citations in book/quarto/contents/vol2/**/*.qmd to [@li2020federated] (or whichever canonical key is preferred). If keeping for some reason, at minimum add explicit year = {2020}.

verdicts.issue: booktitle = {International Conference on Learning Representations} — should be expanded with (ICLR).

missing pages — NeurIPS 2020. The paper has page numbers in the proceedings; could be added. publisher 'Curran Associates Inc.' — minor stylistic, the canonical form per bib-check.md §3 is 'Curran Associates' (no Inc.).

silent corruption: key/title indicates the AWQ MLSys 2024 Best Paper (Lin, Tang, Tang, Yang, Xiao, Han) but year = {2025}, journal = {GetMobile: Mobile Computing and Communications}, and DOI 10.1145/3714983.3714987 all point to a January 2025 GetMobile republication, not the original MLSys 2024 paper. Citations rendering as 'Lin et al., 2025' for the AWQ paper are wrong; the canonical citation is MLSys 2024 (Best Paper). The booktitle = {Proceedings of Machine Learning and Systems} is left over from a partial edit.

suggested_fix.note: Fix in-place: set year = {2024}; replace journal = {GetMobile: ...} with booktitle = {Proceedings of Machine Learning and Systems (MLSys)}; publisher = {mlsys.org}; URL = https://proceedings.mlsys.org/paper_files/paper/2024/hash/42a452cbafa9dd64e9ba4aa95cc1ef21-Abstract-Conference.html; remove the GetMobile DOI or move to a separate note. Type should remain @inproceedings (was @article).

verdicts.issue: no DOI/URL; book exists (Edward Elgar 2023) but no independent locator stamped.
minor: missing explicit year field; only date = {2019}. IEEE Access Vol 7 = 2019.
booktitle = {International Conference on Machine Learning} — should be expanded with (ICML).
minor: missing explicit year field; only date = {2020-06}.

booktitle is the long DBLP form 'Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 30: Annual Conference ... December 4-9, 2017, Long Beach, CA, USA' — should be normalized to 'Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)' per venue-expansion rule (and address removed).

year mismatch and silent corruption: arXiv ID 2601.05047 = January 2026, not 2024 — the YYMM convention puts this paper firmly in 2026. The actual paper 'Challenges and Research Directions for Large Language Model Inference Hardware' by Xiaoyu Ma and David Patterson was posted to arXiv in January 2026. Key year 2024 vs actual year 2026 is a 2-year drift that triggers the key/content year check.

suggested_fix.note: Fix: set year = {2026}; rename key to ma2026challenges; rewrite all [@ma2024challenges] / @ma2024challenges citations in book/quarto/contents/**/*.qmd.

verdicts.issue: minor: missing explicit year field; only date = {2024-04-27}. Booktitle is the verbose ASPLOS form — should be normalized to include (ASPLOS) per venue-expansion.
minor: missing explicit year field (only date = {2017-06-19}). Duplicate of madry2018towards (arXiv preprint vs ICLR 2018 conference).
booktitle = {17th USENIX Symposium on Networked Systems Design and Implementation (NSDI 20)} — has the (NSDI 20) parenthetical but spec prefers (NSDI) without year. Minor.
minor: missing explicit year field; only date = {2020-06}.
minor: missing explicit year field; only date = {2021-10}. Has unusually long keywords field — cosmetic.
minor: missing explicit year field; only date = {2022-06}. Long keywords field — cosmetic.
minor: missing explicit year field; only date = {2022-03-30}.

key uses unusual CamelCase + underscore form 'Masanet_2020' (inconsistent with bib convention). Also a duplicate of masanet2020energy (same DOI 10.1126/science.aba3758, same authors, same title). The underscore in the key is not a LaTeX problem but breaks the lowercase-snake convention used elsewhere. Missing explicit year field (only date = {2020-02-28}).

suggested_fix.note: Delete this duplicate; rewrite any [@Masanet_2020] citations in book/quarto/contents/**/*.qmd to [@masanet2020energy]. If keeping for some reason, at minimum normalize the key to lowercase masanet2020 and add explicit year = {2020}.

verdicts.issue: duplicate of Masanet_2020; this is the better-named key. Missing explicit year field (only date = {2020-02-28}).
minor: missing explicit year field; only date = {2023-10-05}. journal = {ArXiv preprint} with leading capital is unusual; standardize to {arXiv preprint arXiv:2310.03715} format used elsewhere.

publisher = {mlsys.org} is acceptable but bib-check.md §3 prefers no specific publisher requirement for MLSys. booktitle 'Proceedings of Machine Learning and Systems' should add (MLSys) parenthetical.

title = {EPISTEMOLOGICAL PROBLEMS OF ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE} is in ALL CAPS — likely as imported from Crossref. BibTeX will render this as Title Case after sentence-case conversion via Quarto's biblio-style, but for safety, recase as {Epistemological Problems of Artificial Intelligence}.
duplicate of mcmahan2017communicationefficient (same paper, McMahan et al. AISTATS 2017). Both keys retained for prose citation flexibility; consider consolidating.
duplicate of mcmahan2017communication. Same paper. Consider consolidating into one canonical key.
author = {Mead, C.} uses initials only — Carver Mead has a publicly known full first name. Per §4a should be {Mead, Carver} or {Mead, Carver A.}. Minor.

title = {Dremel} alone with the rest in subtitle = {interactive analysis of web-scale datasets} (lowercase). The full title is 'Dremel: Interactive Analysis of Web-Scale Datasets'. Title-Case the subtitle and merge for cleaner rendering.
missing pages — NeurIPS 2019 paper has page numbers in proceedings. third author should be 'Neubig, Graham' (the bib has 'Neumann, Graham' which is a typo — Graham Neubig is the actual third author).

suggested_fix.note: Fix author typo in-place: 'Neumann, Graham' -> 'Neubig, Graham'. No .qmd propagation.

verdicts.issue: silent corruption (Shewhart-class): the key 'miller2015remote' indicates Miller & Valasek's 'Remote Exploitation of an Unaltered Passenger Vehicle' (Black Hat USA 2015). The author field correctly says Miller, Charlie and Valasek, Chris. BUT the title field says 'The Antivirus Hacker's Handbook' (a book by Joxean Koret/Bachaalany), the publisher is Wiley, and the DOI 10.1002/9781119183525.ch15 resolves to the 'Remote Exploitation' chapter of that Wiley book — by Koret and Bachaalany, NOT Miller/Valasek. The DOI/title/publisher belong to a different work; only the author field reflects the intended citation. Citations rendering as 'Miller and Valasek (2015) The Antivirus Hacker's Handbook' are wrong on every level.

suggested_fix.note: Fix in-place to the actual Miller & Valasek work: title = {Remote Exploitation of an Unaltered Passenger Vehicle}; remove DOI 10.1002/9781119183525.ch15 (that's a different paper); url = {http://illmatics.com/Remote%20Car%20Hacking.pdf}; howpublished = {Black Hat USA 2015}; remove publisher = {Wiley}, isbn, issn fields. Type should be @misc or @techreport (Black Hat is not formally peer-reviewed). No .qmd propagation.

verdicts.issue: minor: journal field has '&' HTML entity ('IEEE Design \& Test') — should be '\&' or '{\&}' in BibTeX. The '&' will render literally.
minor: missing explicit year field; only date = {1997-07}.
author = {Mitzenmacher, M.} — Michael Mitzenmacher's full first name is well-attested. Per §4a should be {Mitzenmacher, Michael}.
minor: missing explicit year field; only date = {2015-02-25}.

publisher = {IEEE Comput. Soc} uses abbreviation 'Comput. Soc' — normalize to 'IEEE Computer Society' or 'IEEE'. Authors use initials only (Mohanram, K. and Touba, N.A.) — Kartik Mohanram and Nur A. Touba have full first names.
minor: missing explicit year field (only date = {2018-12}). journal = {Energy Research \& Social Science} has '&' HTML entity — same fix as miller2019lessons.
key year 2016 vs date = {2015-11-14} (arXiv) and CVPR conference year 2016 (DOI confirms). 1-year arxiv-vs-conf gap is silent. Missing explicit year field (only date).

booktitle is the verbose form 'Proceedings of the 13th USENIX Symposium on Operating Systems Design and Implementation (OSDI)' — already has (OSDI) parenthetical. journal = {OSDI} field is redundant given booktitle; consider removing.
author = {{Message Passing Interface Forum}} — corporate author, key prefix 'mpi' is intentionally project/standard name (not surname). Skip surname check per spec.
booktitle = {11th International Symposium on High-Performance Computer Architecture} should add (HPCA) parenthetical per venue-expansion rule.

key year 2023 vs date = {2022-08-23} (DOI shows online-first August 2022, journal volume 2023 issue). The journal 'AI and Ethics' Vol 3 No 3 is published in 2023 even though the article first appeared online in 2022. Year drift is ambiguous: the citable year is typically the journal-issue year (2023), so the key is probably correct. Missing explicit year field (only date). Confirm whether 2022 (online-first) or 2023 (issue) is the canonical citation year for this journal.
minor: missing explicit year field; only date = {2022-01}. Journal of Systems and Software Vol 183 (2022).
